page: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a virtualized server instance belonging to the Dalv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs, which map to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. System memory is limited to 4.0 GB, providing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This server does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, though it is Premium Storage capable. No GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The high clock speed of the AMD EPYC processor enables strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance in both suites falls into the bottom 25% due to the low physical core count. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck; read-only small block cached bandwidth is poor at 151.31 GB/sec, and large block uncached read bandwidth is weak at 37.22 GB/sec, while large block write bandwidth is average at 27.76 GB/sec.

In real-world application benchmarks, the server shows moderate efficiency for single-threaded operations. Gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, registering 29.15 GB/sec and 13.44 GB/sec respectively. However, database and memory-intensive operations are constrained. Passmark database operations rank in the bottom 10%, and Redis SET operations are weak at 2.95 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also weak at 3.44 GB/sec, and LLM inference is limited to small-model text generation at average speeds.

Qualitatively, the D2als_v6 represents a budget-friendly tier for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without high memory or storage demands. The primary tradeoff is the highly constrained 4.0 GB memory capacity and the lack of local storage. This configuration is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, utility scripting, and simple background tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU cycles but do not require large memory footprints or high multi-threaded throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a low-memory, compute-oriented server from the Dalv6 family, featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) running at 3.7 GHz and 4.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, placing in the top 25% for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, though multi-core and memory bandwidth results are generally weak. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average throughput, while database and Redis operations perform poorly due to memory and core constraints. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and utility tasks requiring dedicated CPU execution without high memory or storage requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a Dalv6-family server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. It offers strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 features 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU AMD EPYC server with a low-memory footprint, optimized for single-threaded tasks and lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Exhibits weak multi-core and memory bandwidth performance due to hardware constraints
Suitable for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and utility scripts
